questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Chalk and Talk method?
options: To engage students through interactive discussion
questions.explanation: The Chalk and Talk method is designed to engage students through interactive discussion and questions, promoting active learning.

questions.question: What is the main feature of a PowerPoint presentation?
options: It is a traditional lecture method
It is an interactive discussion method
It is a visual aid with text, images, and videos
questions.explanation: A PowerPoint presentation is a visual aid that uses text, images, and videos to convey information to the audience.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a clear and concise title on a PowerPoint slide?
options: To provide a brief summary of the content
questions.explanation: A clear and concise title on a PowerPoint slide helps the audience quickly understand the content and focus their attention.

questions.question: What is a common mistake in creating a PowerPoint presentation?
options: Not checking for spelling and grammar errors
questions.explanation: One common mistake is not using images or videos to break up text and make the presentation more engaging.

questions.question: What is the benefit of providing handouts or notes after a PowerPoint presentation?
options: To provide a distraction for the presenter
To increase the length of the presentation
To help students review and prepare for a test
questions.explanation: Providing handouts or notes after a PowerPoint presentation helps students review and prepare for a test, and can also clarify any unclear points from the presentation.
